key_topics: Ratios and Proportional Relationships: understanding ratios, unit rates
Ratio tables, tape diagrams, double number lines
Percent as a rate per 100
The Number System: dividing fractions by fractions
Fluency with multi-digit division and multi-digit decimal operations
Greatest common factor (GCF) and least common multiple (LCM)
Positive and negative numbers: rational numbers on the number line
Absolute value and ordering rational numbers
Expressions and Equations: writing and evaluating algebraic expressions
Identifying parts of an expression (terms, factors, coefficients)
Writing equations and inequalities for situations
Dependent and independent variables
Geometry: area of triangles, special quadrilaterals, polygons
Surface area using nets of 3D figures
Volume of rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths
Statistics and Probability: statistical questions, distribution, measures of center
Mean, median, mode, range
Box plots, histograms, dot plots
competences: Understand and apply ratios and unit rates to solve problems
Divide fractions by fractions using the standard algorithm
Write, evaluate, and solve one-variable equations and inequalities
Calculate surface area and volume of 3D figures
Analyze statistical data using measures of center and variability
typical_mistakes: Errors dividing fractions (not flipping the divisor before multiplying)
Errors finding surface area (missing faces of 3D figures)
Misinterpreting absolute value (confusing with negative sign)

key_topics: Reading Literature: theme development, character analysis, author's choices
Point of view and its effect on meaning and tone
Analyzing how author's word choice shapes tone and meaning
Reading Informational Text: central idea, summarizing, author's argument
Evaluating arguments and claims, distinguishing fact from opinion
Comparing texts in different formats (print and multimedia)
Vocabulary: context clues, figurative language, connotation
Writing: argumentative writing with clear claims and evidence
Informative/explanatory writing with organized paragraphs and citations
Narrative writing: narrative techniques, pacing, and word choice
Research: gathering, evaluating, and citing sources (MLA basics)
Language: pronouns and their antecedents, varying sentence patterns, punctuation
competences: Write argumentative essays with claims, evidence, and counterarguments
Analyze how authors develop theme and character
Distinguish facts from opinions in informational texts
Conduct research and cite sources appropriately
typical_mistakes: Confusing claim and evidence in argumentative writing
Not addressing counterarguments in persuasive essays
Using informal language in formal writing
Plagiarizing rather than paraphrasing research sources

key_topics: Waves: electromagnetic spectrum, visible light
Information technologies and instrumentation
Energy: thermal energy, heat transfer
Growth and development of organisms: cell division, DNA replication (intro)
Ecosystems: changes in ecosystems, biodiversity and ecosystem stability
Earth's systems: biogeology, feedback mechanisms
Weather and climate: factors affecting climate, climate change evidence
competences: Describe how electromagnetic waves transfer energy
Explain cellular processes related to growth and reproduction
typical_mistakes: Thinking all cells divide at the same rate
Misunderstanding biodiversity's role in ecosystem stability

key_topics: Ancient civilizations: Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome (world history approach)
Political systems: early democracy (Athens), republic (Rome)
Geography of ancient world: rivers, trade routes, agricultural foundations
World religions: origins and beliefs of major religions
Economic systems: ancient trade, currency, specialization
Fall of Rome and transition to medieval period
Map skills: latitude, longitude, map projections, time zones
competences: Compare political systems of ancient civilizations
Trace trade routes using historical maps
Identify major world religions and their core beliefs
Analyze primary sources from ancient civilizations
typical_mistakes: Not understanding how geography shaped ancient civilizations
